feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Suspected case of hantavirus in Ontario County on Thursday
entry.summary: Ontario County Public Health (OCPH) said it's investigating a possible case of locally-acquired hantavirus. OCPH says there's no connection to the current cruise ship infections, and no risk to the general public.

entry.title: CDC says there are no U.S. hantavirus cases currently, 41 people being monitored
entry.summary: The CDC said the risk to the general public from hantavirus remains low.

entry.title: What happens to your brain under anesthesia?
entry.summary: New findings challenge the widespread belief that anesthesia is simply a deep sleep. The reality is more complex, researchers found.

entry.title: How to make your home smell nice without adding indoor air pollution
entry.summary: Experts share how to get that fresh feeling without overdoing it on home fragrances and the particulates they release into the air.

entry.title: Man Quarantining After Hantavirus Outbreak Speaks Out: ‘I Was In A Really Dark Place’
entry.summary: Jake Rosmarin said he’s “trying not to think about” whether he’s been infected with hantavirus as he hunkers down for six weeks in a medical facility.

entry.title: How to stay safe from hantavirus, norovirus as summer travel season approaches
entry.summary: While the global threat for hantavirus remains low, there is still a chance you could be exposed to the virus while you're traveling.

entry.title: RFK Jr. Takes Part in Open Heart Surgery in Bonkers Move
entry.summary: Donald Trump’s health secretary fiddled with robotic surgical arms as a patient lay on the operating table.

entry.title: Are you an optimist? It may help protect your heart.
entry.summary: A growing body of research suggests that optimism may protect the heart — both biologically and by promoting healthier habits.

entry.title: I Got an Accutane Nose Job
entry.summary: Severe acne led me to take isotretinoin for eight months. I walked away with clear skin and a slimmer nose.

entry.title: If You’re Over 50, Here’s How Much Protein You Actually Need
entry.summary: Protein needs increase with age. Learn how much protein adults over 50 need daily, plus dietitian-approved tips to eat more protein.

entry.title: New Calif. study finds evidence cannabis could treat obesity, diabetes
entry.summary: A new study from California scientists found that cannabis oil had surprising effects when given to obese mice, including reversing signs of diabetes.

entry.title: Engaging with arts and culture can slow biological aging as much as exercise, study suggests
entry.summary: When it comes to slowing down our biological aging, engaging with arts and culture is as beneficial as physical activity, a new study suggests.

entry.title: Eating One Egg a Day Could Cut Alzheimer’s Risk by 27%
entry.summary: Scientists tracking more than 40,000 people for over 15 years found an intriguing connection between egg consumption and reduced Alzheimer’s risk.

entry.title: Hantavirus cluster linked to cruise ship travel, Multi-country
entry.summary: This is the third Disease Outbreak News report on the hantavirus cluster, following the notification to the World Health Organization (WHO) on 2 May 2026 of severe respiratory illness cases aboard MV Hondius, a cruise ship. Since the last DON was published on…

entry.title: CDC Is Walking a Tightrope With Its Response to Hantavirus
entry.summary: Administration officials host frequent calls and have reallocated staffing, but don’t want to replicate Covid-era response

entry.title: PNW doctor from hantavirus ship taken to special isolation unit
entry.summary: An oncologist traveling on a cruise ship amid a hantavirus outbreak says he's the lone American isolated at a special biocontainment unit in Nebraska.

entry.title: Ecotypes of triple-negative breast cancer in response to chemotherapy
entry.summary: Treatment data for triple-negative breast cancer show the importance of macrophage subtypes and cancer-cell metaprograms for interferon signalling, HLA expression and cell cycle activity that are associated with a good response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy.

entry.title: Sustaining microglial reparative function enhances stroke recovery
entry.summary: Reparative microglia persist in the brain after stroke but become dysfunctional through ZFP384-mediated mechanisms; however, this process can be mitigated by targeting Zfp384 using therapeutic antisense oligonucleotides.

entry.title: Long-term editing of brain circuits using an engineered electrical synapse
entry.summary: Connexin proteins found in white perch fish were used to engineer synthetic electrical synapses, enabling precision circuit editing in mammals.

entry.title: AI is fabricating citations in biomedical studies, researchers find
entry.summary: "Your doctor could be making decisions around treatment based on studies that never existed," one expert said.

entry.title: Connecticut Confirms First Case Of Clade I Mpox
entry.summary: The person had recently traveled to Western Europe, according to state health officials.

entry.title: Why Men and Women Hear Differently
entry.summary: Researchers present work on sex-dependent auditory variability, showing how hormones influence hearing loss and processing.

entry.title: A new kind of liver crisis is emerging in the U.S. The American diet is to blame
entry.summary: Young people and women are particularly vulnerable to MetALD, a dangerous merging of metabolic disease and alcohol use.

entry.title: A New Genetic Entry Point for Autism Traits
entry.summary: A new study identifies PTCHD1-AS as a key non-coding gene that shapes social and repetitive behaviors in autism without affecting cognition.

entry.title: Sleep linked to slower ageing: huge study pinpoints the right amount
entry.summary: Health outcomes were better in people who slept between about six and eight hours a day.

entry.title: 3 new cases of measles reported in Lancaster County
entry.summary: Lancaster County has three new cases of measles, the state Department of Health announced today. Additionally, the department reported a new measles case in Berks County – the first for

entry.title: Obesity has risen in all countries — but at a faster pace in poorer ones
entry.summary: Trajectories of obesity prevalence over the past 45 years reveal patterns of growth, plateau and decline that differ across high-, middle- and low-income countries.

entry.title: Critically ill French hantavirus patient is on an artificial lung as outbreak grows to 11
entry.summary: The French passenger hospitalized in Paris has a severe form of the disease that has caused life-threatening lung and heart problems, said Dr. Xavier Lescure, an infectious disease specialist at Bichat Hospital.

entry.title: Highly Contagious Human Parvovirus Circulating In Northern CA: What To Know
entry.summary: Health officials emphasize preventative steps as the seasonal outbreak peaks across California cities.

entry.title: Opinion | 11 hantavirus deaths in Argentina were a warning
entry.summary: Deadly viruses will slip through, but there are ways to boost prevention.

entry.title: New Yorker describes hantavirus quarantine experience after cruise ship outbreak: "Trying to stay positive"
entry.summary: A New York native is among 16 American passengers who are quarantining in Nebraska after being on the cruise ship that is at the center of the deadly hantavirus outbreak.

entry.title: The silent vitamin deficiency experts say could increase your cancer risk over time
entry.summary: There is a reason doctors keep repeating the same advice across generations: eat balanced meals, avoid shortcuts, and do not ignore what the body is trying to say. Nutrients may look small on paper, but inside the body, they perform some of the most delicate …

entry.title: MN resident possibly exposed to Hantavirus
entry.summary: This story has been corrected to reflect the following: The Minnesota resident was exposed to a person who was on the MV Hondius cruise ship and tested positive for the virus. SIOUX FALLS, S.D. (KELO) — One person in Minnesota has possibly been exposed to the…

entry.title: This daily habit could lower dementia risk by 35%, scientists say
entry.summary: A huge long-term study found that drinking two to three cups of coffee a day was linked to a much lower risk of dementia, especially before age 75. Researchers say caffeine may help keep brain cells active while reducing inflammation and harmful plaque buildu…

entry.title: Woman isolating on British island in South Pacific after hantavirus contact
entry.summary: The woman who was on a virus-hit ship shows "no signs of illness", the government of Pitcairn Islands, a British Overseas Territory, says.

entry.title: American Who Wasn’t On Doomed Cruise Is Feared to Have Caught Rat Virus
entry.summary: Officials do not believe the man contracted the Andes strain linked to the cruise ship outbreak.

entry.title: How does diet impact brain health? Alzheimer's Association shares nutrition tips with cognitive function in mind.
entry.summary: Alzheimer's Association CEO and president Joanne Pike shares some recommendations on which foods to eat, and which foods to limit, for better brain health. (Sponsored by the Alzheimer's Association.)

entry.title: Cruise ship passenger making best of quarantine in US after hantavirus outbreak
entry.summary: When Jake Rosmarin boarded the MV Hondius, he gleefully posted on social media that the ship would be his home for 35 days as he traveled across the South Atlantic. Now, he is one of 18 Americans u…

entry.title: Americans All Negative For Hantavirus, French Patient Critically Ill (Live Updates)
entry.summary: The American who tested positive for hantavirus says he is symptom free while a patient in Paris is critically ill.

entry.title: 'Forever Chemicals' Found in 98.8% of Human Blood Samples Tested
entry.summary: The 'forever chemicals' previously linked to multiple health harms really are everywhere: They've now been detected in 98.8 percent of 10,566 blood samples tested in a new US study.

entry.title: We’re asking the wrong question about the hantavirus outbreak
entry.summary: Hantavirus probably won’t become a pandemic. But the way we cover outbreaks like this one — and the system being dismantled behind the scenes — should worry you.

entry.title: AI can design viruses, toxins and other bioweapons. How worried should we be?
entry.summary: Scientists are debating whether to limit biological AI software to ward off threats.

entry.title: PCOS Is Officially Renamed, After Decades of Misinformation
entry.summary: First, it was named after two men.

entry.title: I'm A 41-Year-Old Single Mom. I'm Over Dating Men My Own Age.
entry.summary: “I didn’t always date younger men. In fact, just after my divorce at age 33, I actively avoided it.”

entry.title: Scientists link boy’s tumor to gene therapy viruses, in rare finding
entry.summary: Doctors have linked a boy's tumor to a gene therapy, but say the rare risks need to be weighed against the profound benefits such medicines can offer.

entry.title: How to navigate your menopause
entry.summary: From coping with hot flushes to understanding brain fog, there’s plenty you can do to help yourself through menopause

entry.title: Scientists design an obesity drug that hits five metabolic targets at once, raising hopes beyond today’s injections
entry.summary: Scientists developed a five-target obesity drug that outperformed Ozempic in weight loss and blood sugar control in obese mice.

entry.title: You should probably eat more fiber. Here's why — and how to do it : Short Wave
entry.summary: The average person eats 10-15 grams of fiber per day, according to the USDA. The problem? That’s WAY under the recommended daily amount. Fiber – a type of carb that our bodies are unable to digest – is prevalent in foods like fruits, veggies, whole grains, an…

entry.title: A Common Vitamin Has a Complicated Link to Cancer, Experts Reveal
entry.summary: We've all heard the advice: eat your fruit and vegetables, get your vitamins, and stay healthy.

entry.title: Is hantavirus a pandemic risk? Medical experts discuss comparison to COVID-19
entry.summary: Imagery of health officials in full personal protective equipment quarantining people from a Dutch cruise ship has driven memories of the coronavirus pandemic. WCPO asked, is there a risk?
